MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gilbert L. Purcell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Marjorie Roberts, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT BAKERSFIELD PIPE AND SUPPLY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Bakersfield Pipe and Supply, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent William Roberts was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and decedent's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, they identified persons with knowledge of decedent's exposure to defendant's asbestos-containing products and defendant did not demonstrate that the identified persons do not have such knowledge. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Defendant's evidence is insufficient to demonstrate that (1) plaintiffs have identified the wrong entity, and/or (2) the gaskets purchased at the store at issue did not contain asbestos. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, decedent's deposition testimony and the declarations of Victor Figueroa and Keith Roussell would create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' objections to Dan Byrum's declaration are overruled as to objection no. 1, sustained as to objection no. 2 only as to his statement "and review of BPS corporate records," and sustained as to objection nos. 3 and 4. If the Court got to plaintiffs' evidence, defendant's objections to the declarations of Victor Figueroa and Keith Roussell would be overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
